html:
Exclusive to:

GenericHappyUnhappy

The Antlion is a Mob exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in A New Reign. It appears in the Desert biome during Summer Season. The players can either appease or kill it, otherwise, it creates Sinkholes on the surface or Cave-in Boulder when a player goes inside the caves. The Antlion can be given a Thermal Stone at stage 1 to freeze it and start combat, or one at stages 4 or 5 to set it on fire and start combat (using a hot Thermal Stone requires you to heat it by Antlion since it will not stay hot when away from fire during Summer, making this impractical).

Contents

  • 1 Behavior
    • 1.1 Tribute
    • 1.2 Sinkhole
  • 2 Fight
    • 2.1 Sand Castles and Sand spikes
    • 2.2 Tanking
    • 2.3 Kiting
    • 2.4 Abigail
  • 3 Trivia
  • 4 Assets
  • 5 Gallery
  • 6 Sounds

Behavior

Antlion first spawns in Summer when there is no rain and the ground is dry. 33 minutes and 36 seconds (roughly four days) after it spawns it will disturb the players unless tribute is paid to delay it. When the attack is complete, Antlion's disturbance timer will be set to 80% of the previous calm duration.

If it rains while the Antlion is Alive, it will despawn, respawning later with a reset timer at 33 minutes 36 seconds when the rain stops.

Tribute

Players can contribute the following items to delay the Antlion's disturbance:

Days Items
1/3
2/3
1
2
3
4
6

For example, if one gives the Antlion an Egg, it would delay its attacks for 160 seconds, or 1/3 day. Giving the Antlion 10 Rocks will delay the disturbance timer by 3.3 days. One should keep in mind that its disturbance timer cannot exceed 6 days, so players may need to contribute a hefty amount of items to it at least twice throughout Summer in order to never be attacked, if players are going the appeasement route.

When the Antlion is given a trinket or an egg it will give a Desert Stone. If it is specifically given a Beach Toy it will give a blueprint for The Lazy Deserter as well as the Turf-Raiser Helm but no Desert Stone. If it receives a Red Crown, it will give blueprints for Brick Flooring, Gold Flooring and Humble Lamb Idol.

The Antlion has a maximum tribute period of 6 days. Once the maximum tribute is given, the Antlion performs a belly-rubbing animation with calm growls, indicating it is satisfied. This animation serves as a reminder for players to offer the next tribute within the following 6 in-game days, or 48 minutes, to avoid triggering destructive Sinkholes and Cave-in Boulder.

The easiest way to pay tribute is to mine rocks commonly found in the Mosaic biome, Deserts or underground stalagmites. The player could end up paying up to 6 Cut Stone or 18 rocks at a time, resulting in around 40 rocks per summer to keep the Antlion satisfied.

When the timer is up, there will be 3 warnings to let the players know that Sinkholes or Cave-in Boulder will start to spawn soon. Up to 2 players can be targeted by the Antlion in a single attack with between 2-5 Sinkholes if on the surface or 6-10 boulders if in the Caves spawning around each of them.

Sinkhole

For Sinkholes leading to the Caves world, see Sinkhole.

Sinkholes are craters that are created by the Antlion during summer when displeased. When they are created, they damage players and destroy Structures where they form. Once formed, new structures cannot be built on them and they'll slow down all players walking on them by 70%. Sinkholes cannot be removed by the player and will only begin to self-repair after 21-22 days since they were formed, taking 9-11 more days to fully repair. Players can prevent them from appearing by contributing items to the Antlion (listed below) or by killing it.

Characters also say different quotes depending on how long before the Sinkholes happen.

Fight

Sand SpikeSand Castle

The Antlion can be given a stage 1, 4 or 5 Thermal Stone to start combat. The Antlion will either freeze or smolder, and then attack the nearest player within 3.75 Tiles. The Antlion will then attempt to trap the player within Sand Castles (not to be confused with Sand Castles featured in the Shipwrecked DLC), and then repeatedly slam into the ground, causing Sand Spikes to jut out of the ground. The Antlion can heal 200 each time it eats rocks. The healing can be interrupted by attacking the Antlion.

If killed, the Antlion drops 4 Meat, 6–8 Desert Stones, Blueprints for the The Lazy Deserter and Turf-Raiser Helm, 2–4 Rocks and varying amounts of Trinkets. The desert stone that is dropped may be insufficient until the following summer, and hence players may want to obtain a good amount of Desert Stones before killing the Antlion. Killing Antlion will ensure the summer will no longer have any disturbance from Antlion.

There are multiple strategies for killing Antlion. Any of them are best with 2 thermal stones (one for Antlion and one for you) and appropriate goggles (for vision and movement). Build a fridge or endothermic fire several tiles away to cool the stones before combat (too close and antlion will destroy them). Start with full health and sanity. Restorative food such as Surf 'n' Turf or Pierogi and any necessary light should complete your preparations.

Sand Castles and Sand spikes

The Sand Spikes and Sand Castle, in increasing sizes, has 25, 65, 100, 250 HP. They last for 6-7, 7-8, 8-10, 15-16 seconds. Any structures placed/built that are hit by Sand Castles or Sand Spikes will be destroyed. Sand Castles are a way of trapping you, and the Sand Spikes are a way of damaging you, dealing either 50, 75, or 100 damage on hit, increasing with the size of the Sand Spike. Mobs take double damage. The Sand Castles and Sand Spikes can be turned into decorative Glass Castles and Glass Spikes when set on fire.

Tanking

A single player should be able to tank Antlion with approximately 1.5 Marble Suits and 0.5 Thulecite Clubs by holding down attack and only occasionally maneuvering closer when pushed out of melee range by the sand spikes. Keep the attack as continuous as practical so that Antlion cannot heal (the club's tentacles help with this).

Alternatively, a player could light 29 Gunpowders while Antlion is frozen to drop its health down below 200, drastically shortening the time it takes to kill it while still giving its loot.

Kiting

Players are likely to need Desert Goggles or Astroggles, paired with unrestrictive body armor such as a Log Suit or Thulecite Suit. Players can dodge the upcoming Sand Spike, using the sand circle as indicators for their locations. Players also can move preemptively since antlion would always target at least 1 attacker with sand spike. Players should move around to attack antlion from all angles to avoid a dense packing of spikes that will trap the players.

Below is the number of hits it takes with each weapon to kill a Antlion when playing with characters with a default damage modifier. The Weather Pain, Brightshade Staff, and Bee Mine are not included due to their random natures. This table does not take into account the damage bonus gained from characters with a lunar or shadow affinity. It also does not take into account the 40% damage bonus Wendy gets from attacking the same mob as Abigail, Wanda's damage bonus with Shadow weapons (except for the Alarming Clock, which does accurately reflect that bonus), or Wolfgang's damage multiplier when he isn't mighty.

Abigail

When playing as Wendy , it's possible to use Abigail to defeat the boss. Abigail can kill the Antlion on her own when riled up. As a floating creature, she is completely immune to its sand spike attacks. Giving her a Nightshade Nostrum will significantly speed up the process.

Wendy should stay outside the sandcastle ring and refrain from direct combat to make avoiding sand spike attacks much easier.

Remember that Abigail can sometimes lose focus, especially if there are other hostile or neutral mobs nearby, so it is advised to clear the area of such creatures before starting the fight. If that happens, the Wendy player should immediately redirect her target using the “Attack At” command to prevent the Antlion from healing.


Trivia

  • The Antlion was introduced in the Against the Grain update as a trader, while the ability to fight it was added in the Heart of the Ruins update.
  • The Antlion's name is a pun on its appearance (Ant + Lion) and the fly-like insect, the Antlion.
  • Wickerbottom and Maxwell's quote for the Desert Stone suggests that the Antlion might have come into contact with gems before, since its bezoars are impure Orange Gems.
  • Maxwell's quote for the Antlion is a pun on the word pride, referencing the fact that a group of lions is called a pride.
  • According to Wickerbottom, its scientific name is "Panthera auropunctata". This alludes to the combination of the scientific names of the two animals that make the Antlion; the genus of the lion "Panthera leo" and the specific epithet of the little fire ant "Wasmannia auropunctata".
  • When the Antlion was first released, several characters referred to it as a female. These examination quotes were later changed to a gender-neutral "it".
    • However, Wigfrid, whose quotes for the Antlion were changed to be gender-neutral, refers to the Antlion as "her" when examining the Antlion Figure, which was added long after the quotes were changed.
      • Warly, who was introduced to Don't Starve Together after the quotes were changed, refers to the Antlion as "amie", the French word for a female friend, when paying tribute. However, it appears that Warly uses amie and its masculine counterpart ami interchangeably: Wolfgang and Wormwood are also referred to as amie by him.
  • There's a value in the code that shows that the sinkholes could be repairable, but currently do not have any tool assigned to be able to fix them.
  • The Antlion and Dragonfly spawn in different desert biomes. Antlion's desert contains the Oasis and limbed Cacti, while Dragonfly's desert has Spiky Trees, Hound Mounds, and round cacti. Both have flintless boulders, Spiky Bushes, and Buzzards however. Hound mounds CAN spawn in Antlion's desert if a special Set Piece generates.
  • If Antlion is spawned using the Console in any Season other than Summer, it will disappear after being spawned.

The players can either appease or kill it, otherwise, it creates Sinkholes on the surface or Cave-in Boulder when a player goes inside the caves. Sinkholes are craters that are created by the Antlion during summer when displeased. When they are created, they damage players and destroy Structures where they form. Once formed, new structures cannot be built on them and they'll slow down all players walking on them by 70%. Sinkholes cannot be removed by the player and will only begin to self-repair after 21-22 days since they were formed, taking 9-11 more days to fully repair. Players can prevent them from appearing by contributing items to the Antlion (listed below) or by killing it.
Characters also say different quotes depending on how long before the Sinkholes happen.

“Woah! How do you do that?”
–Walter
“The sands of time are so fickle!”
–Wanda
The Antlion can be given a stage 1, 4 or 5 Thermal Stone to start combat. The Antlion will either freeze or smolder, and then attack the nearest player within 3.75 Tiles. The Antlion will then attempt to trap the player within Sand Castles (not to be confused with Sand Castles featured in the Shipwrecked DLC), and then repeatedly slam into the ground, causing Sand Spikes to jut out of the ground. The Antlion can heal 200 each time it eats rocks. The healing can be interrupted by attacking the Antlion.
If killed, the Antlion drops 4 Meat, 6–8 Desert Stones, Blueprints for the The Lazy Deserter and Turf-Raiser Helm, 2–4 Rocks and varying amounts of Trinkets. The desert stone that is dropped may be insufficient until the following summer, and hence players may want to obtain a good amount of Desert Stones before killing the Antlion. Killing Antlion will ensure the summer will no longer have any disturbance from Antlion.
There are multiple strategies for killing Antlion. Any of them are best with 2 thermal stones (one for Antlion and one for you) and appropriate goggles (for vision and movement). Build a fridge or endothermic fire several tiles away to cool the stones before combat (too close and antlion will destroy them). Start with full health and sanity. Restorative food such as Surf 'n' Turf or Pierogi and any necessary light should complete your preparations.
Sand Castles and Sand spikes[edit | edit source]
The Sand Spikes and Sand Castle, in increasing sizes, has 25, 65, 100, 250 HP. They last for 6-7, 7-8, 8-10, 15-16 seconds. Any structures placed/built that are hit by Sand Castles or Sand Spikes will be destroyed. Sand Castles are a way of trapping you, and the Sand Spikes are a way of damaging you, dealing either 50, 75, or 100 damage on hit, increasing with the size of the Sand Spike. Mobs take double damage. The Sand Castles and Sand Spikes can be turned into decorative Glass Castles and Glass Spikes when set on fire.
Tanking[edit | edit source]
A single player should be able to tank Antlion with approximately 1.5 Marble Suits and 0.5 Thulecite Clubs by holding down attack and only occasionally maneuvering closer when pushed out of melee range by the sand spikes. Keep the attack as continuous as practical so that Antlion cannot heal (the club's tentacles help with this).
Alternatively, a player could light 29 Gunpowders while Antlion is frozen to drop its health down below 200, drastically shortening the time it takes to kill it while still giving its loot.
Kiting[edit | edit source]
Players are likely to need Desert Goggles or Astroggles, paired with unrestrictive body armor such as a Log Suit or Thulecite Suit. Players can dodge the upcoming Sand Spike, using the sand circle as indicators for their locations. Players also can move preemptively since antlion would always target at least 1 attacker with sand spike. Players should move around to attack antlion from all angles to avoid a dense packing of spikes that will trap the players.
Below is the number of hits it takes with each weapon to kill a Antlion when playing with characters with a default damage modifier. The Weather Pain, Brightshade Staff, and Bee Mine are not included due to their random natures. This table does not take into account the damage bonus gained from characters with a lunar or shadow affinity. It also does not take into account the 40% damage bonus Wendy gets from attacking the same mob as Abigail, Wanda's damage bonus with Shadow weapons (except for the Alarming Clock, which does accurately reflect that bonus), or Wolfgang's damage multiplier when he isn't mighty.

Abigail[edit | edit source]
When playing as Wendy , it's possible to use Abigail to defeat the boss. Abigail can kill the Antlion on her own when riled up. As a floating creature, she is completely immune to its sand spike attacks. Giving her a Nightshade Nostrum will significantly speed up the process.
Wendy should stay outside the sandcastle ring and refrain from direct combat to make avoiding sand spike attacks much easier.
Remember that Abigail can sometimes lose focus, especially if there are other hostile or neutral mobs nearby, so it is advised to clear the area of such creatures before starting the fight. If that happens, the Wendy player should immediately redirect her target using the “Attack At” command to prevent the Antlion from healing.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The Antlion was introduced in the Against the Grain update as a trader, while the ability to fight it was added in the Heart of the Ruins update.
The Antlion's name is a pun on its appearance (Ant + Lion) and the fly-like insect, the Antlion.
Wickerbottom and Maxwell's quote for the Desert Stone suggests that the Antlion might have come into contact with gems before, since its bezoars are impure Orange Gems.
Maxwell's quote for the Antlion is a pun on the word pride, referencing the fact that a group of lions is called a pride.
According to Wickerbottom, its scientific name is "Panthera auropunctata". This alludes to the combination of the scientific names of the two animals that make the Antlion; the genus of the lion "Panthera leo" and the specific epithet of the little fire ant "Wasmannia auropunctata".
When the Antlion was first released, several characters referred to it as a female. These examination quotes were later changed to a gender-neutral "it".
However, Wigfrid, whose quotes for the Antlion were changed to be gender-neutral, refers to the Antlion as "her" when examining the Antlion Figure, which was added long after the quotes were changed.
Warly, who was introduced to Don't Starve Together after the quotes were changed, refers to the Antlion as "amie", the French word for a female friend, when paying tribute. However, it appears that Warly uses amie and its masculine counterpart ami interchangeably: Wolfgang and Wormwood are also referred to as amie by him.
There's a value in the code that shows that the sinkholes could be repairable, but currently do not have any tool assigned to be able to fix them.
The Antlion and Dragonfly spawn in different desert biomes. Antlion's desert contains the Oasis and limbed Cacti, while Dragonfly's desert has Spiky Trees, Hound Mounds, and round cacti. Both have flintless boulders, Spiky Bushes, and Buzzards however. Hound mounds CAN spawn in Antlion's desert if a special Set Piece generates.
If Antlion is spawned using the Console in any Season other than Summer, it will disappear after being spawned.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Kiến Sư Tử là một Mob độc quyền của Don't Starve Together, được giới thiệu trong A New Reign. Nó xuất hiện trong Quần xã Sa mạc trong Mùa Hè. Người chơi có thể xoa dịu hoặc giết nó, nếu không, nó sẽ tạo ra Hố sụt trên bề mặt hoặc Tảng đá Hang động khi người chơi đi vào trong hang. Kiến Sư tử có thể được cấp Đá Nhiệt ở giai đoạn 1 để đóng băng nó và bắt đầu chiến đấu, hoặc một viên ở giai đoạn 4 hoặc 5 để đốt cháy và bắt đầu chiến đấu (sử dụng Đá Nhiệt nóng yêu cầu bạn phải làm nóng nó bằng Antlion vì nó sẽ không nóng khi ở xa lửa trong Mùa hè, khiến điều này không thực tế).

You must be behind all the seismic activity. |woodie = They grow'em big out here, eh? |maxwell = Have you no pride? |wigfrid = Tis the mighty liön! |webber = You're fuzzy. We like that. |winona = How's the weather up there? |warly = I think it's friendly. |wortox = You need only tell me what you desire. |wormwood = Hello! |wurt = What you want? |walter = Hi! |wanda = Oh botheration, I hope I remembered to bring a gift. |choose = wigfrid }} |-|Happy= {{Quotes |wilson = I think we're on good terms. |willow = You're in a good mood. |wolfgang = Monster does not look hungry today. |wendy = The gifts made it happier... |wx78 = IT'S REPULSIVELY HAPPY |wickerbottom = It has been mollified with tributes. |woodie = It seems agreeable. |maxwell = We've placated it for now. |wigfrid = The liön smiles upön us. |webber = It's always nice to have more bug friends. |winona = Looks like we're safe for awhile. |warly = Life is good right now. |wortox = Are you not pleased, my friend? |wormwood = Happy Roar Bug! |wurt = She look much happier! |walter = We're becoming pals already. |wanda = Oh now you're in a good mood? |choose = wigfrid }} |-|Unhappy= {{Quotes |wilson = It looks mad. |willow = There's gonna be tremors in our future. |wolfgang = Monster is looking very scary! |wendy = A deep rage brews within... |wx78 = VOLATILE ORGANIC EMOTIONS DETECTED |wickerbottom = Panthera auropunctata is looking irritable. |woodie = That critter's in a real bad mood. |maxwell = It's going to wreak havoc on my world. |wigfrid = We've incurred its wrath! |webber = I think it's upset. |winona = That is not a happy monster. |warly = Oh no, don't be mad. |wortox = Apologies, dear beast! I am but a worm! |wormwood = Oh no. Needs gifts |wurt = Glorp, don't be mad! |walter = It's hard to read her expression... |wanda = There's no need to get yourself so worked up! |choose = wigfrid |source = when the Antlion is unhappy or in combat }} The '''Antlion''' is a [[Mobs|Mob]] exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', introduced in ''[[A New Reign]]''. It appears in the [[Desert]] biome during [[Seasons/Summer|Summer]] Season. The players can either appease or kill it, otherwise, it creates Sinkholes on the surface or [[Cave-in Boulder]] when a player goes inside the caves. The Antlion can be given a [[Thermal Stone]] at stage 1{{pic|24|Thermal_Stone_Stage_1}} to freeze it and start combat, or one at stages 4{{pic|24|Thermal Stone Stage 4}} or 5{{pic|24|Thermal Stone Stage 5}} to set it on fire and start combat (using a hot Thermal Stone requires you to heat it by Antlion since it will not stay hot when away from fire during Summer, making this impractical). =={{Pic32|Brain}} Behavior== Antlion first spawns in Summer when there is no rain and the ground is dry. 33 minutes and 36 seconds (roughly four days) after it spawns it will disturb the players unless tribute is paid to delay it. When the attack is complete, Antlion's disturbance timer will be set to 80% of the previous calm duration. If it rains while the Antlion is Alive, it will despawn, respawning later with a reset timer at 33 minutes 36 seconds when the rain stops. ===Tribute=== Players can contribute the following items to delay the Antlion's disturbance: {| class="wikitable" !Days !Items |- | style="text-align: center;" |1/3 |{{Pic32|Rocks}}{{Pic32|Egg}}{{Pic32|Second-hand Dentures}}{{Pic32|Bent Spork}}{{Pic32|Air Unfreshener}}{{Pic32|Leaky Teacup}}{{Pic32|Shoe Horn}} {{pic32|Collected Dust}} |- | style="text-align: center;" |2/3 |{{Pic32|Melty Marbles}}{{Pic32|Gord's Knot}}{{Pic32|Tiny Rocketship}}{{Pic32|Ball and Cup}}{{Pic32|Frayed Yarn}}{{Pic32|Wire Hanger}}{{Pic32|White Bishop}}{{Pic32|Black Bishop}}{{Pic32|White Rook}}{{Pic32|Black Rook}}{{Pic32|White Knight}}{{Pic32|Black Knight}}{{Pic32|Gnome}}{{Pic32|Gnomette}}{{Pic32|Frazzled Wires}}{{Pic32|Lying Robot}}{{Pic32|Beaten Beater}}{{Pic32|Fake Kazoo}}{{Pic32|Toy Trojan Horse}}{{Pic32|Unbalanced Top}} |- | style="text-align: center;" |1 |{{Pic32|Cut Stone}}{{Pic32|Mismatched Buttons}}{{Pic32|Back Scratcher}}{{Pic32|Hardened Rubber Bung}}{{Pic32|Dessicated Tentacle}}{{Pic32|Lucky Cat Jar}}{{Pic32|Potato Cup}} |- | style="text-align: center;" |2 |{{Pic32|Thermbell}}{{Pic32|Red Crown}} |- | style="text-align: center;" |3 |{{Pic32|Beach Toy}} |- | style="text-align: center;" |4 |{{Pic32|Thermal Stone Stage 2|Thermal Stone/DST}} {{Pic32|Heat Stone Stage 3|Thermal Stone/DST}} |- | style="text-align: center;" |6 |{{Pic|32|Guard Tower Core}}{{Pic|32|Spark Socket}}{{Pic|32|Cranky Articulator}} |} For example, if one gives the Antlion an [[Egg]], it would delay its attacks for 160 seconds, or 1/3 day. Giving the Antlion 10 [[Rocks]] will delay the disturbance timer by 3.3 days. One should keep in mind that its disturbance timer cannot exceed 6 days, so players may need to contribute a hefty amount of items to it at least twice throughout Summer in order to never be attacked, if players are going the appeasement route. When the Antlion is given a trinket or an egg it will give a [[Desert Stone]]. If it is specifically given a [[Beach Toy]] it will give a blueprint for [[The Lazy Deserter]] as well as the [[Turf-Raiser Helm]] but no Desert Stone. If it receives a [[Red Crown]], it will give blueprints for [[Brick Flooring]], [[Gold Flooring]] and [[Humble Lamb Idol]]. The Antlion has a maximum tribute period of 6 days. Once the maximum tribute is given, the Antlion performs a belly-rubbing animation with calm growls, indicating it is satisfied. This animation serves as a reminder for players to offer the next tribute within the following 6 in-game days, or 48 minutes, to avoid triggering destructive Sinkholes and [[Cave-in Boulder]]. The easiest way to pay tribute is to mine rocks commonly found in the Mosaic biome, Deserts or underground stalagmites. The player could end up paying up to 6 [[Cut Stone]] or 18 rocks at a time, resulting in around 40 rocks per summer to keep the Antlion satisfied. When the timer is up, there will be 3 warnings to let the players know that Sinkholes or Cave-in Boulder will start to spawn soon. Up to 2 players can be targeted by the Antlion in a single attack with between 2-5 Sinkholes if on the surface or 6-10 boulders if in the Caves spawning around each of them. ===Sinkhole=== {{Object Infobox |name= Sinkhole |image = Sinkhole Cracks.png |spawnCode = "antlion_sinkhole"}}{{For|Sinkholes leading to the [[Caves]] world|Sinkhole}} {{Quotes |wilson = The ground is destabilizing! |willow = The ground's cracking! |wolfgang = Ground is crumble beneath might of Wolfgang! |wendy = The ground crumbles beneath me. |wx78 = STRUCTUAL INTEGRITY COMPROMISED |wickerbottom = The ground will soon give way. |woodie = Pothole incoming. |maxwell = My world is crumbling! |wigfrid = The earth rebels against us! |webber = The ground's giving way! |winona = Earthquake! I think? |warly = Was that my stomach rumbling? |wortox = Tremors and trembles! |wormwood = Sinking! |wurt = Why ground shaking?! |walter = Hang on, I'm sure my handbook says what to do in an earthquake! |wanda = What is that horrible rumble? |choose = wendy |source = when announcing a sinkhole.}} '''Sinkholes''' are craters that are created by the Antlion during summer when displeased. When they are created, they damage players and destroy [[Structures]] where they form. Once formed, new structures cannot be built on them and they'll slow down all players walking on them by 70%. Sinkholes cannot be removed by the player and will only begin to self-repair after 21-22 days since they were formed, taking 9-11 more days to fully repair. Players can prevent them from appearing by contributing items to the Antlion (listed below) or by killing it. Characters also say different quotes depending on how long before the Sinkholes happen. =={{Pic|32|Spear}} Fight== {{Object Infobox |name = Sand Spike |image = Sand Spike Tall.png|Tall Sand Spike Medium.png|Medium Sand Spike Small.png|Small |damage = 50 / 75 / 100 to player
100 / 150 / 200 to mobs |health = 25 / 65 / 100 |spawnCode = "sandspike" }} {{Object Infobox |name = Sand Castle |image = Sand Castle Antlion.png |health = 250 |spawnCode = "sandblock" }} |-|Sand Spike= {{Quotes |wilson = I could've been skewered! |willow = Burn it! |wolfgang = Spike is made of sand! |wendy = Aim for my heart... |wx78 = MY CIRCUITS ARE GOING TO GET DUSTY |wickerbottom = Panthera auropunctata is misbehaving today. |woodie = That's something else, eh? |maxwell = That was quite impolite. |wigfrid = The earth itself dares tö fight me! |webber = Ouchie! |winona = Hit and a miss! |warly = Watch yourself, it's quite sharp. |wortox = Hyuyu, you won't get me! |wormwood = Oh. Ouch! |wurt = Aaah! Spiky! |walter = Hey, careful! You almost hit me with that! |wanda = I won't have my time cut short by the likes of you! |choose = wilson }} |-|Sand Castle= {{Quotes |wilson = It's so gritty! |willow = Buuurn! |wolfgang = Is very sandy. |wendy = Mockery of a spoiled childhood. |wx78 = DO NOT GET SAND IN MY CIRCUITS |wickerbottom = Quite a phenomenal talent! |woodie = Some good craftsmanship. |maxwell = Impressive. |wigfrid = Tis a castle öf sand! |webber = Not like this!! |winona = Things are getting gritty! |warly = Very thick sand. |wortox = Goodness gracious, goodness me! |wormwood = Can play with it? |wurt = Gloorph! |walter = Woah! How do you do that? |wanda = The sands of time are so fickle! |choose = wilson }} The Antlion can be given a stage 1, 4 or 5 [[Thermal Stone]] to start combat. The Antlion will either freeze or smolder, and then attack the nearest player within 3.75 [[Tile]]s. The Antlion will then attempt to trap the player within Sand Castles (not to be confused with [[Sand Castle]]s featured in the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC), and then repeatedly slam into the ground, causing Sand Spikes to jut out of the ground. The Antlion can heal 200 {{hp}} each time it eats rocks. The healing can be interrupted by attacking the Antlion. If killed, the Antlion drops 4 [[Meat]], 6–8 [[Desert Stone]]s, [[Blueprint]]s for the [[The Lazy Deserter]] and [[Turf-Raiser Helm]], 2–4 [[Rocks]] and varying amounts of [[Trinkets]]. The desert stone that is dropped may be insufficient until the following summer, and hence players may want to obtain a good amount of Desert Stones before killing the Antlion. Killing Antlion will ensure the summer will no longer have any disturbance from Antlion. There are multiple strategies for killing Antlion. Any of them are best with 2 thermal stones (one for Antlion and one for you) and appropriate goggles (for vision and movement). Build a fridge or endothermic fire several tiles away to cool the stones before combat (too close and antlion will destroy them). Start with full health and sanity. Restorative food such as [[Surf 'n' Turf]] or [[Pierogi]] and any necessary light should complete your preparations. ===Sand Castles and Sand spikes=== The Sand Spikes and Sand Castle, in increasing sizes, has 25, 65, 100, 250 HP. They last for 6-7, 7-8, 8-10, 15-16 seconds. Any structures placed/built that are hit by Sand Castles or Sand Spikes will be destroyed. Sand Castles are a way of trapping you, and the Sand Spikes are a way of damaging you, dealing either 50, 75, or 100 damage on hit, increasing with the size of the Sand Spike. Mobs take double damage. The Sand Castles and Sand Spikes can be turned into decorative [[Glass Castle]]s and [[Glass Spike]]s when set on fire. ===Tanking=== A single player should be able to tank Antlion with approximately 1.5 [[Marble Suit]]s and 0.5 [[Thulecite Club]]s by holding down attack and only occasionally maneuvering closer when pushed out of melee range by the sand spikes. Keep the attack as continuous as practical so that Antlion cannot heal (the club's tentacles help with this). Alternatively, a player could light 29 [[Gunpowder]]s while Antlion is frozen to drop its health down below 200, drastically shortening the time it takes to kill it while still giving its loot. === Kiting=== Players are likely to need [[Desert Goggles]] or [[Astroggles]], paired with unrestrictive body armor such as a [[Log Suit]] or [[Thulecite Suit]]. Players can dodge the upcoming Sand Spike, using the sand circle as indicators for their locations. Players also can move preemptively since antlion would always target at least 1 attacker with sand spike. Players should move around to attack antlion from all angles to avoid a dense packing of spikes that will trap the players. {{Weapon Hits DST|health=6000}} === Abigail === When playing as [[Wendy]] {{Pic|26|Wendy Portrait}}, it's possible to use [[Abigail]] to defeat the boss. Abigail can kill the Antlion on her own when riled up. As a floating creature, she is completely immune to its sand spike attacks. Giving her a [[Ghostly Elixir#Nightshade Nostrum|Nightshade Nostrum]] will significantly speed up the process. Wendy should stay outside the sandcastle ring and refrain from direct combat to make avoiding sand spike attacks much easier. Remember that Abigail can sometimes lose focus, especially if there are other hostile or neutral mobs nearby, so it is advised to clear the area of such creatures before starting the fight. If that happens, the Wendy player should immediately redirect her target using the “Attack At” command to prevent the Antlion from healing. =={{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia== *The Antlion was introduced in the ''[[A New Reign#February 2, 2017 - Against the Grain|Against the Grain]]'' update as a trader, while the ability to fight it was added in the ''[[A New Reign#February 23, 2017 - Heart of the Ruins (Part 1)|Heart of the Ruins]]'' update. *The Antlion's name is a pun on its appearance (Ant + Lion) and the fly-like insect, the [[Wikipedia:Antlion|Antlion]]. *Wickerbottom and Maxwell's quote for the Desert Stone suggests that the Antlion might have come into contact with gems before, since its bezoars are impure Orange Gems. *Maxwell's quote for the Antlion is a pun on the word pride, referencing the fact that a group of lions is called a pride. *According to Wickerbottom, its scientific name is "''Panthera auropunctata''". This alludes to the combination of the scientific names of the two animals that make the Antlion; the genus of the lion "''[[Wikipedia:Panthera leo|Panthera leo]]''" and the specific epithet of the little fire ant "''[[Wikipedia:Wasmannia auropunctata|Wasmannia auropunctata]]''". *When the Antlion was first released, several characters referred to it as a female. These examination quotes were later changed to a gender-neutral "it". **However, [[Wigfrid]], whose quotes for the Antlion were changed to be gender-neutral, refers to the Antlion as "her" when examining the [[Chess Pieces#Antlion Figure|Antlion Figure]], which was added long after the quotes were changed. ***[[Warly]], who was introduced to ''Don't Starve Together'' after the quotes were changed, refers to the Antlion as "amie", the French word for a female friend, when paying tribute. However, it appears that Warly uses amie and its masculine counterpart ami interchangeably: [[Wolfgang]] and [[Wormwood]] are also referred to as amie by him. *There's a value in the code that shows that the sinkholes could be repairable, but currently do not have any tool assigned to be able to fix them. *The Antlion and [[Dragonfly]] spawn in different desert biomes. Antlion's desert contains the [[Lake|Oasis]] and limbed [[Cactus|Cacti]], while Dragonfly's desert has [[Spiky Tree]]s, [[Hound Mound]]s, and round [[Cactus|cacti]]. Both have [[Boulder#Flintless|flintless boulders]], [[Spiky Bush]]es, and [[Buzzard]]s however. Hound mounds CAN spawn in Antlion's desert if a special [[Set_Piece#Hound_Fortress|Set Piece]] generates. *If Antlion is spawned using the [[Console]] in any [[Seasons|Season]] other than [[Seasons/Summer|Summer]], it will disappear after being spawned. {{Clear}} == {{Pic32|Research Notes}} Assets == Antlion Attack.gif|Attack Antlion Appear.gif|Appear Antlion Sleep.gif|Sleep Antlion Taunt.gif|Taunt Antlion Disappear.gif|Disappear Antlion Death.gif|Death Antlion Eating.gif|Eating =={{Pic32|Blueprint}} Gallery== Antlion Sprites.png|Antlion Sprites Antlion Sprites2.png|Antlion Sprites Antlion Screenshot.png|[[Wes]] standing next to the Antlion.
